title: pycnandra benthamii
intro: Pycnandra benthamii, also known as the "mara" tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sarcolataceae that is native to New Caledonia. Pycnandra benthamii has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. Pycnandra benthamii is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, and is also used to make traditional medicine, including a type of oil that is applied topically to the skin.
